title: Module 1
content:
Introduction, categories of ships, various sectors of international marine
industry. Types of trade and methods of employment of ships.
Economics in ship design, economic criteria, shipbuilding cost, operating
cost, required freight rate. Owner’s requirements.
Phases of ship design, design spiral. Ship parameters, deadweight,
displacement, deadweight coefficient, hold capacity, stowage factor.

title: Module 2
content:
Weight equation and solution. Volume equation and solution. Basic design
procedure for main categories of ships, deadweight carriers, capacity
carriers, linear dimension ships, special purpose ships. Procedure for
selection of main dimensions (length, breadth, depth, draught) and form
coefficients. Estimation of weight, lightship weight, steel weight,
machinery weight, outfit weight, deadweight.
General Arrangement (GA); factors influencing GA; subdivision of ship’s
hull; superstructure and deckhouses; arrangement of cargo spaces; cargo
handling arrangement; arrangement of machinery spaces, accommodation
and associated spaces; tanks, and other spaces.

title: Module 3
content:
Ship’s lines plan, conventional method of lines design, lines design using
existing lines plan, lines design using standard series, hull form section
shapes, form of bow, bulbous bow, form of stern. Effect of hull form on
ship’s performance.

title: Module 4
content:
Freeboard and load line regulations. Stability and trim, watertight integrity.
IMO regulations, intact stability criteria, damage stability criteria. Stability
booklet. Tonnage regulations, gross tonnage, net tonnage.
